company_profile.name: ADMA Biologics, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: ADMA Biologics, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company, develops, manufactures, and markets specialty plasma-derived biologics for the treatment of immune deficiencies and infectious diseases in the United States and internationally. The company operates through ADMA BioManufacturing and Plasma Collection Centers segments. The company offers BIVIGAM, an intravenous immune globulin (IVIG) product indicated for the treatment of primary humoral immunodeficiency (PI); ASCENIV, an IVIG product for the treatment of PI; and Nabi-HB, a human polyclonal antibody product for the treatment of acute exposure to blood containing Hepatitis B surface antigen and other listed exposures to Hepatitis B. It also develops a pipeline of plasma-derived therapeutics comprising products related to the methods of treatment and prevention of S. pneumonia infection for an immunoglobulin. In addition, the company operates source plasma collection facilities. The company sells its products through independent distributors, drug wholesalers, specialty pharmacies, and other alternate site providers. ADMA Biologics, Inc. was incorporated in 2004 and is headquartered in Ramsey, New Jersey.
company_profile.description_summary:
This biopharmaceutical company develops, manufactures, and markets specialty plasma-derived biologics treating immune deficiencies and infectious diseases. It generates revenue primarily through BIVIGAM and ASCENIV, intravenous immune globulin products for primary immunodeficiency, plus Nabi-HB for Hepatitis B exposure treatment. The company also operates plasma collection facilities.

The company distinguishes itself through its integrated business model combining biomanufacturing with owned source plasma collection centers, supporting its product portfolio and pipeline development in immunoglobulin therapeutics.

_notes.revenue_vs_net_income: This chart shows how much money the company brings in (revenue) versus how much it actually keeps as profit (net income) each year. A healthy company should show both lines growing over time. If revenue grows but net income shrinks, the company is spending more than it is earning — a warning sign worth investigating.
_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.

company_profile.name: Adaptive Biotechnologies Corporation
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Adaptive Biotechnologies Corporation, a commercial-stage company, develops an immune medicine platform for the diagnosis and treatment of various diseases. The company offers immunosequencing platform which combines a suite of proprietary chemistry, computational biology, and machine learning to generate clinical immunomics data to decode the adaptive immune system. It provides clonoSEQ diagnostic test which detects and monitors the remaining number of cancer cells that are present in a patient's body during and after treatment, known as Minimal Residual Disease (MRD). The company offers products and services for life sciences research, clinical diagnostics, and drug discovery applications. The company was formerly known as Adaptive TCR Corporation and changed its name to Adaptive Biotechnologies Corporation in December 2011. Adaptive Biotechnologies Corporation was incorporated in 2009 and is headquartered in Seattle, Washington.
company_profile.description_summary:
Develops an immune medicine platform using immunosequencing technology that combines proprietary chemistry, computational biology, and machine learning to decode the adaptive immune system. Generates revenue primarily through clonoSEQ, a diagnostic test that detects and monitors minimal residual disease in cancer patients, alongside products and services for life sciences research, clinical diagnostics, and drug discovery.

The company's distinctive immunosequencing platform leverages proprietary technology and advanced computational methods to create clinical immunomics data, enabling applications across diagnostics and therapeutic development that competitors cannot easily replicate.

company_profile.name: Analog Devices, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Cyclical business (technology) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
company_profile.description: Analog Devices, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, testing, and marketing of integrated circuits (ICs), software, and subsystems products in the United States, rest of North and South America, Europe, Japan, China, and rest of Asia. It provides data converter products, which translate real-world analog signals into digital data, as well as translates digital data into analog signals; power management and reference products for power conversion, driver monitoring, sequencing, and energy management applications in the automotive, communications, industrial, and consumer markets; and power ICs that include performance, integration, and software design simulation tools for accurate power supply designs. The company also offers amplifiers to condition analog signals; and radio frequency and microwave ICs to support cellular infrastructure; and micro-electro-mechanical systems technology solutions, including accelerometers used to sense acceleration, gyroscopes for sense rotation, inertial measurement units to sense multiple degrees of freedom, and broadband switches for radio and instrument systems, as well as isolators. In addition, it provides digital signal processing and system products for numeric calculations. The company serves clients in the industrial, automotive, consumer, instrumentation, aerospace, defense and healthcare, and communications markets through a direct sales force, third-party distributors, and independent sales representatives, as well as online. The company was incorporated in 1965 and is headquartered in Wilmington, Massachusetts.
company_profile.description_summary:
Designs and manufactures integrated circuits, software, and subsystems that convert analog signals to digital data and vice versa. Primary revenue comes from data converters, power management products, amplifiers, and radio frequency ICs serving automotive, industrial, communications, and consumer markets globally through direct sales, distributors, and online channels.

Distinctive capabilities include specialized sensor technologies like accelerometers and gyroscopes, digital signal processing products, and comprehensive power supply design tools that enable customers to optimize complex electronic systems across aerospace, defense, and healthcare applications.

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (technology) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

quality.note: Moat scored by Haiku API — final_score_pct computed out of 50.

quality_narrative.moat_rationale: ADI has a moderate competitive moat built on solid profitability and strong margins, but weak returns on invested capital and cyclical industry exposure limit its ability to command pricing power or create lasting competitive advantages.

metric_assessments.profitability: A 26% profit margin puts ADI well above the 10–15% range typical of industrial and semiconductor companies, showing the business converts revenue into earnings efficiently. The company's high-margin analog chips and signal processing products command premium pricing because they solve specialized problems that competitors struggle to replicate cheaply. However, margin strength alone doesn't translate to shareholder returns—a 9.64% return on equity sits squarely in the average zone (most quality companies target 12%+), revealing that ADI hasn't yet converted its operational excellence into proportional profits on shareholder capital.
metric_assessments.financial_strength: A current ratio of 2.19 and debt-to-EBITDA of 1.42 indicate ADI has comfortable liquidity and manageable leverage for a cyclical technology company—well within safe operating ranges. The strong balance sheet provides a cushion to weather downturns in the semiconductor cycle and fund development without financial strain. The one limiting factor is that leverage has crept up relative to typical semiconductor peers, which means less room to borrow during future downturns or for acquisitions, though it poses no near-term concern.
metric_assessments.growth: A 37.2% revenue jump signals robust demand, but this likely reflects the post-pandemic semiconductor cycle and recent M&A integration (MaxLinear acquisition) rather than organic market expansion. The projected 29.6% earnings-per-share growth over the next five years is solid, yet it trails the 40%+ growth rates seen in pure-play software or high-growth semiconductor designers, placing ADI squarely in the adequate tier. The core constraint is that analog chip markets are mature and growing low single digits structurally—ADI's above-market growth depends on market share gains and favorable industry cycles that won't last forever.
metric_assessments.predictability: Operating in analog semiconductors provides some pricing stability and recurring customer relationships, supporting the 6/10 score. Large industrial and automotive customers tend to stick with proven suppliers because switching costs are real—redesigning a product around a different chip is expensive and time-consuming. However, ADI remains exposed to cyclical end-market demand (automotive, industrial automation, communications), meaning earnings can swing sharply even with steady market share, which is why the score stops at good rather than excellent.
metric_assessments.valuation: A 2/10 valuation score means the stock is currently expensive relative to earnings and growth prospects. At high forward multiples typical of the semiconductor industry in growth phases, ADI offers little margin of safety for an investor buying today—if growth disappoints or the cycle turns, downside could be sharp. The weakness here is structural: cyclical companies command lower multiples in fair markets because of earnings volatility, yet ADI trades at a premium, leaving minimal room for error.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: ADI earned a Speculative rating because it combines moderate business quality (solid profitability and balance sheet) with cyclical earnings, unproven long-term growth, and elevated valuation. For a beginner, this means the company isn't broken—it generates real profits and has financial staying power—but the stock price already reflects optimistic assumptions about future growth and market cycles. If those assumptions hold, you'll do well; if the semiconductor cycle softens or competition intensifies, the stock could fall significantly, making it a higher-risk bet than a stable, cheaply valued company.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Analog Devices is a well-run semiconductor specialist with an enviable 26% profit margin and a fortress balance sheet, commanding premium prices in niche analog and mixed-signal markets that are harder for competitors to crack. However, the business faces a structural headwind: analog chip markets grow slowly, meaning ADI's above-market growth depends on favorable semiconductor cycles that eventually reverse, and its strong recent earnings reflect both operational excellence and cyclical tailwinds that likely won't persist. The company's projected 30% five-year earnings growth is respectable but not exceptional for a growth stock, while its current valuation offers no margin of safety—the market is already pricing in years of strength. For conservative beginners, the combination of cyclical exposure, unproven secular growth, and a premium stock price makes this a speculative bet best suited to investors comfortable with volatility and willing to wait for a sharper price decline before entering.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Analog Devices, Inc. demonstrates mixed fundamental quality and benefits from solid financial strength.
quality_narrative.risk_profile: Key risks to watch: a valuation premium of 49% above estimated intrinsic value.
quality_narrative.investment_summary: With 29.6% consensus EPS growth forecast, the growth outlook supports the investment case. The stock trades at a 49% premium to estimated intrinsic value and is appropriate for investors with higher risk tolerance and conviction in the growth thesis.

company_profile.name: Aditxt, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Aditxt, Inc. focuses on discovery and development of biopharmaceutical products. The company develops AditxtScore and FlowSpot that enable individuals to understand, manage, and monitor their immune profiles in order to be informed about attacks on or by their immune system; and Apoptotic DNA Immunotherapy, a nucleic acid-based technology that utilizes an approach that mimics the way the body naturally induces tolerance to its tissues. It develops ADI-100 for skin grafting, psoriasis, and type-1 diabetes. In addition, the company provides Pearsanta that develops molecular tests based on the mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) to develop tests for early detection of cancer; Mitomic targets mutations in mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) to detect disease; Mitomic prostate test a blood-based assay that quantifies the level of the 3.4kb mtDNA deletion; and Mitomic endometriosis test, a blood-based assay that quantifies the level of one or more mtDNA deletions. Further, it offers Adivir for commercial development of products to address significant unmet needs in infectious disease and population health; and Adifem address critical unmet needs in women health. The company has a license agreement with Loma Linda University and Leland Stanford Junior University. The company was formerly known as ADiTx Therapeutics, Inc. and changed its name to Aditxt, Inc. in July 2021. Aditxt, Inc. was incorporated in 2017 and is headquartered in Mountain View, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
Aditxt develops biopharmaceutical products and diagnostic tests centered on immune profiling and mitochondrial DNA analysis. Its revenue-generating platforms include AditxtScore and FlowSpot for immune monitoring, diagnostic tests through Pearsanta and Mitomic for cancer and disease detection, plus therapeutic candidates like ADI-100 for autoimmune conditions.

The company distinctively combines immunotherapy with mitochondrial DNA-based diagnostics, leveraging apoptotic DNA technology that mimics natural immune tolerance mechanisms alongside blood-based assays detecting disease-specific mtDNA deletions.

company_profile.description: Advanced Biomed Inc., an investment holding company, engages in the research and development of microfluidic biochip technologies for precision oncology detection, diagnosis, and treatment in Taiwan. Its devices include A+Pre, an automated sample preparation system that enables pre-concentration of diluted blood samples; AC-1000, a rare cell device to solve the problem of detection of hypercoagulable state samples of tumor patients; A+SCDrop, a circulating tumor cell (CTC) single-cell capture device; and A+CellScan, an analyzer with an immunostaining chip for immunostaining/analysis of the AC-1000 product. The company also offers microfluidic biochips, such as A+Pre, AC-1000 CTC enrichment, and A+CellScan chips to provide assay products and services to cancer patients. In addition, its immunochromogenic kits include A+CTCE kit to identify epithelial circulating tumor cells and determined by the immunochromogenicity of EpCAM, CK (pan), CD45, and CD14 antibodies; A+CTCM kit to identify mesenchymal circulating tumor cells based on the immunochromogenicity of Vimentin, CD45, and CD14 antibodies; A+EMT to identify epithelial-to-mesenchymal circulating tumor cells determined by the immunochromogenicity of cell-surface Vimentin, and CD45 and CD14 antibodies; and A+CM to identify tumor-associated macrophages based on the immunochromogenicity of PD-L1, CD45, and CD14 antibodies. Further, the company is developing A+LCGuard for early screening of lung cancer. Additionally, it establishes and operates medical clinics in the People's Republic of China. The company was founded in 2014 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

It also operates medical clinics in China and develops emerging applications like lung cancer screening tools.

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (technology) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 40.8%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

quality_narrative.moat_rationale: Autodesk has built a strong competitive moat through industry-standard software with high switching costs, exceptional profitability (50.4% ROE), and predictable recurring revenue, though its growth constraint and moderate valuation score suggest the market isn't pricing in unlimited pricing power.

metric_assessments.profitability: A 50.4% return on equity is exceptional—most strong companies achieve 15–20%—and a 19.5% profit margin is well above the 10–12% typical for software businesses, showing Autodesk converts revenue into earnings far more efficiently than peers. The company's design and construction software has created a moat where customers find switching to competitors costly and disruptive. What's holding it back from a perfect 10 is that this profitability, while outstanding, isn't growing faster than the business itself, suggesting the company has already optimized pricing and isn't expanding margins further.
metric_assessments.financial_strength: A 1.27 debt-to-EBITDA ratio is moderate and manageable for a profitable software company—well below the 3.0–4.0 that signals financial stress—and the business generates enough cash to service obligations comfortably. However, a current ratio of 0.85 is below the 1.0 benchmark, meaning short-term liabilities exceed short-term assets, which creates a minor liquidity concern if the business faced unexpected pressures. For a free-cash-flow compounder with strong profitability, this is acceptable, but it prevents a higher score since it leaves less immediate cushion than ideal.
metric_assessments.growth: Revenue growing at 18.4% annually is strong for a mature software company and well above typical enterprise software growth of 10–12%, signaling continued market demand and successful product expansion. With earnings projected to compound at 15.9% over five years, the company is delivering reliable, above-market growth. The constraint here is the company's own growth limitation (flagged in the data), meaning management or market conditions are capping expansion potential—it's not growing as fast as it could, preventing a 10/10 despite solid fundamentals.
metric_assessments.predictability: An 8/10 reflects the highly recurring nature of Autodesk's subscription-based business model, where customer churn is low and revenue is contracted well in advance, making earnings forecasts reliable. The combination of strong profitability (10/10) and 18.4% revenue growth with 15.9% EPS growth shows the company consistently translates top-line expansion into bottom-line results without surprises. The small gap to perfect predictability likely stems from the growth constraint noted in the data—external factors or self-imposed limits are introducing some uncertainty about the pace of future expansion.
metric_assessments.valuation: At a 6/10, the stock is fairly valued but not cheap; the market is pricing in the company's strong quality and growth, leaving limited margin of safety for new investors. A quality score of 85% combined with 15.9% EPS growth would typically command a premium, so a 6/10 valuation suggests you're paying a reasonable price for what you're getting rather than catching a bargain. This is appropriate for a compounder of Autodesk's caliber—it's not a screaming buy, but it's not overpriced for a business this well-run.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Autodesk earned a Safe rating because it combines exceptional profitability and earnings quality (50.4% ROE, 19.5% margins) with predictable, subscription-based revenue and strong growth, reducing the risk of earnings disappointments or business deterioration. The main constraint is financial strength sitting at 7/10 due to a below-1.0 current ratio and moderate debt levels, which prevents a higher safety grade, and a growth constraint that caps upside potential—meaning the business is rock-solid and low-risk, but not a home-run opportunity at today's valuation.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Autodesk is a high-quality software business that dominates design and engineering workflows across construction, manufacturing, and media, generating exceptional returns on equity (50.4%) and converting nearly 20% of revenue into profit—far better than typical software peers. The recurring subscription model creates loyal customers with high switching costs, and 18.4% revenue growth is strong for a mature industry leader, supporting projected 15.9% annual earnings growth over the next five years. The main risks are a current ratio below 1.0 (a minor liquidity concern), moderate leverage, and an internal or external growth constraint that limits how fast the company can expand. At a fair valuation (not cheap, not expensive), the stock is suitable for long-term investors seeking quality and steady growth, but it lacks the margin of safety that makes for a compelling bargain-priced opportunity. For patient, quality-focused investors comfortable with market-rate valuations, Autodesk is a solid core holding rather than a compelling buy.

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (energy) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

company_profile.description: Afya Limited operates as a medical education group in Brazil. The company operates in three segments: Undergraduate, Continuing Education, and Medical Practice Solutions. The Medical Practice Solutions segment offers clinical decision support platforms, medical practice management software, healthcare financial services, and digital healthcare ecosystem solutions, such as electronic medical records, practice management tools, telemedicine, digital prescriptions, and doctor-patient relationship platforms. The company also offers educational health and medical imaging; and other programs to lifelong medical learners enrolled across its distribution network, as well as to third-party medical schools. In addition, it offers printed and digital content, as well as an online medical education platform and physicians, healthcare professionals and students. Afya Limited was founded in 1999 and is headquartered in Belo Horizonte, Brazil.

company_profile.name: Agenus Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Agenus Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company, discovers and develops immunotherapies for cancer and infectious diseases in the United States and internationally. It offers Retrocyte Display, an antibody expression platform for the identification of fully human and humanized monoclonal antibodies; and display technologies. It develops QS-21 Stimulon adjuvant, a saponin-based vaccine adjuvant. The company also develops Balstilimab, a programmed death receptor-1 (PD-1) blocking antibody; AGEN1181, a human Fc-enhanced cytotoxic T-lymphocyte antigen 4 (CTLA-4) blocking antibody that is in Phase 2 trials in metastatic colorectal cancer (mCRC), pancreatic cancer, and melanoma; AGEN2373, a CD137 monospecific antibody that is in Phase 1b clinical trial; AGEN1423, a CD73/TGFß TRAP antibody; AGEN1571, an ILT2 monospecific antibody that is in clinical development; and BMS-986442, a TIGIT bispecific antibodies. In addition, it develops INCAGN1876, a GITR agonist; INCAGN2390, a TIM-3 monoclonal antibodies; INCAGN2385, a LAG-3 monospecific antibody; MK-4830, a monospecific antibody targeting ILT4 that is in Phase 2 clinical trial; and AGEN1884. The company operates under the Agenus, MiNK, Prophage, Retrocyte Display, and STIMULON trademarks. It has collaborations with Bristol-Myers Squibb Company, Betta Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd., UroGen Pharma Ltd., Gilead Sciences, Inc., Incyte Corporation, and Merck Sharp & Dohme. The company was formerly known as Antigenics Inc. and changed its name to Agenus Inc. in January 2011. Agenus Inc. was founded in 1994 and is headquartered in Lexington, Massachusetts.
company_profile.description_summary:
A clinical-stage biotechnology company discovering and developing immunotherapies for cancer and infectious diseases. It generates revenue through its Retrocyte Display antibody expression platform and QS-21 Stimulon vaccine adjuvant, while advancing multiple pipeline candidates including checkpoint inhibitors and co-stimulatory agonists in clinical trials.

The company distinguishes itself through proprietary display technology platforms and strategic partnerships with major pharmaceutical firms including Bristol-Myers Squibb, Merck, and Incyte, enabling collaborative development of novel immuno-oncology therapies.

company_profile.name: AgomAb Therapeutics NV
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: AgomAb Therapeutics NV, a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, develops novel disease-modifying therapies for immunology and inflammatory diseases in the United States. The company's product pipeline includes Ontunisertib (AGMB-129), an oral gastrointestinal-restricted small molecule inhibitor of ALK5 or TGFßR1 for the treatment of Fibrostenosing Crohn's disease, which is in Phase 2a clinical trials; and AGMB-447, an inhaled small molecule inhibitor of ALK5 or TGFßR1 for the treatment of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis that is in Phase 1 clinical trials. It also develops AGMB-101, a HGF-mimetic monoclonal antibody that acts through agonism or stimulation of the MET receptor and has demonstrated antifibrotic and regenerative activity in preclinical models. The company was incorporated in 2017 and is headquartered in Antwerp, Belgium.
company_profile.description_summary:
This clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company develops novel therapies targeting immunology and inflammatory diseases. Its pipeline includes Ontunisertib for fibrostenosing Crohn's disease in Phase 2a trials, AGMB-447 for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis in Phase 1, and AGMB-101, a HGF-mimetic antibody showing antifibrotic and regenerative potential in preclinical studies. Currently generates no material revenue.

The company's distinctive approach focuses on ALK5/TGFβR1 inhibitors delivered through different routes—oral and inhaled formulations—alongside a complementary MET receptor agonist, targeting fibrotic diseases across multiple organ systems.

_meta.type_rationale: Real estate investment trust — P/NAV (P/B) prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) own properties and are legally required to pay out most of their income as dividends. This means they rarely have much free cash flow left over, so DCF models give misleading results. Instead, we use Price-to-Book (P/B) to estimate the value of the underlying properties, and EV/EBITDA to compare the business to similar property companies.
